description: Stytch provides authentication infrastructure for consumer and B2B applications, along with workspace-management tooling for configuring Stytch projects. It also offers a hosted MCP server for interacting with Stytch workspace configuration through AI tools.
summary: Stytch exposes three documented HTTP APIs—Consumer Authentication, B2B SaaS Authentication, and Programmatic Workspace Actions—plus a hosted remote MCP server at https://mcp.stytch.dev/mcp; the HTTP APIs use Basic auth with Stytch-issued keys, and the MCP server uses OAuth discovered from its well-known metadata.
